untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of: (1) limit order book microstructure state (order age, price deviation from trades, order size percentile ranking), (2) identification of specific limit orders on the book with their timestamps, (3) volatility expansion percentile detection, and (4) information asymmetry signals. These are order-book-level and latency-sensitive conditions that cannot be expressed using only OHLCV-derived indicators. The signal fundamentally requires real-time order book data, limit order tracking, and statistical comparison of order-level properties—not available in standard price/volume/technical indicator space.
translation_notes: Attempted partial translation using volatility expansion (hvol_20 > 60th percentile) and recent price movement as proxies, with +25 bps profit target (0.0025 in decimal). However, the core signal logic—identifying stale limit orders with information disadvantage—requires order book microstructure data not present in the allowed indicator set. A production implementation would require integration with real-time order book feeds and order-level analytics.
